pattern: The reply for every message follows the unique tree-shortest path back, so once each server's round-trip time is known you can compute, in closed form, the last moment it will still be resending before the whole network falls silent.
hints: Run a single BFS from the master (node 0) to get every server's shortest hop distance -- a message's round-trip time is just twice that hop count.
A server keeps resending at multiples of its own patience value only while the reply from its very first send hasn't arrived yet; find the last such resend time before round-trip time elapses.
The network's idle second is one past the LAST message any server ever sends arriving back -- take the maximum, over every server, of (its last send time + its round trip), then add one.

edge_inputs.description: single edge, tight patience forces exactly one resend
star graph, three servers with different patience values
patience at its maximum value so the server never resends
minimum patience value of 1 on a two-hop chain
round trip time is an exact multiple of the patience value
a cycle exists in the network alongside a longer branch
a long chain to exercise larger hop distances

stress.description: random tree of servers (guaranteed connected) with random patience per server
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    edges = []
    for i in range(1, n):
        parent = rng.randint(0, i - 1)
        edges.append([parent, i])
    patience = [0] + [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    return [edges, patience]

oracle_python:
from collections import deque, defaultdict

class Solution:
    def networkBecomesIdle(self, edges, patience):
        n = len(patience)
        graph = defaultdict(list)
        for u, v in edges:
            graph[u].append(v)
            graph[v].append(u)
        dist = [-1] * n
        dist[0] = 0
        q = deque([0])
        while q:
            u = q.popleft()
            for v in graph[u]:
                if dist[v] == -1:
                    dist[v] = dist[u] + 1
                    q.append(v)
        ans = 0
        for i in range(1, n):
            rt = 2 * dist[i]
            p = patience[i]
            k = 0
            last_sent = 0
            while k * p < rt:
                last_sent = k * p
                k += 1
            ans = max(ans, last_sent + rt + 1)
        return ans
